violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, ice machine, ice scoop) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener brought to be cleaned and sanitized. PIC purchased bagged ice to be used until ice machine can be turned off to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025

Working containers of liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -refrigeration gaskets -refrigeration door handles -ice machine. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025
A leak was observed at the drain to the 3-bay sink. PIC stated service has been scheduled. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025
The floor near the dish machine has accumulation of food residue. The area under shelving (back door) has an accumulation of trash debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024
Pipe under 3 bay sink was observed leaking into a bucket. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
